ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) of the product */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the product */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier */,
  "length" REAL /* Length of the product */,
  "width" REAL /* Width of the product */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the product */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the product */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material the product is made of */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the product */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of the product in inventory */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL to an image of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "reorder_point" INTEGER /* Inventory level at which a reorder should be initiated */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity to reorder when the inventory level reaches the reorder point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the warehouse */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the warehouse */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the warehouse */,
  "opening_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the warehouse */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Total capacity of the warehouse in terms of volume or weight */,
  "current_occupancy" REAL /* Current occupancy of the warehouse in terms of volume or weight */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_warehouses_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_warehouse_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in the warehouse */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the quantity was last updated */,
  "shelf_location" TEXT /* Specific location within the warehouse where the product is stored */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_warehouse_mapping_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_warehouse_mapping_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "employee_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the employee (e.g., manager, supervisor, staff) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee belongs to */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "supervisor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's supervisor */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory update */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being updated */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse where the update occurred */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who made the update */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the inventory was updated */,
  "quantity_change" INTEGER /* Change in quantity of the product */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the inventory update */,
  "note" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_updates_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_updates_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_updates_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was placed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "shipping_method" TEXT /* Method of shipping (e.g., standard, express) */,
  "shipping_cost" REAL /* Cost of shipping */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., credit card, PayPal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in the order */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product at the time of the order */,
  "tax" REAL /* Tax applied to the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Shipping address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "preferred_contact_method" TEXT /* Preferred method of contact (e.g., email, phone) */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points accumulated by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipments" (
  "shipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shipment */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order being shipped */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse from which the shipment originates */,
  "ship_date" TEXT /* Date when the shipment was shipped */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for the shipment */,
  "carrier" TEXT /* Carrier used for the shipment (e.g., FedEx, UPS) */,
  "shipping_cost" REAL /* Cost of shipping */,
  "delivery_address" TEXT /* Delivery address for the shipment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipments_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipments_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipment_items" (
  "shipment_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shipment item */,
  "shipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shipment */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in the shipment */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product at the time of the shipment */,
  "tax" REAL /* Tax applied to the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shipment_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipment_items_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipment_items_shipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("shipment_id") REFERENCES shipments ("shipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order from which the product is being returned */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being returned */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product being returned */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date when the product was returned */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  "refund_amount" REAL /* Amount refunded to the customer */,
  "return_condition" TEXT /* Condition of the returned product (e.g., new, used, damaged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_returns" (
  "product_return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product return mapping */,
  "return_id" INTEGER /* ID of the return */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse where the product is returned */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product returned */,
  "return_status" TEXT /* Status of the return (e.g., pending, processed, rejected) */,
  "processing_date" TEXT /* Date when the return was processed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_returns_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_returns_return_id FOREIGN KEY ("return_id") REFERENCES returns ("return_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being reviewed */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who wrote the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment written by the customer */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes indicating the review was helpful */,
  "not_helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes indicating the review was not helpful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_reviews_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_reviews_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categorization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES product_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_category_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product-category mapping */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the category is the primary category for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_category_mapping_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_category_mapping_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES product_categories ("category_id")
)
